reasoning: There is currently no approved vaccine specifically developed for gonorrhea. While the UK NHS recently (August 2025) started rolling out the Meningitis B vaccine (Bexsero) for vulnerable populations due to its cross-protection against gonorrhea, this is a repurposed use and not a new gonorrhea vaccine. The Wikipedia page "List of infectious diseases" listed the vaccine status for Gonorrhea as "No" as of October 16, 2025 (over two months after the UK NHS announcement), meaning the recent news did not result in an update to the table. GSK's dedicated gonorrhea vaccine candidate is still in Phase II clinical trials with long monitoring periods, so it will certainly not be completed and approved by January 2026. For the Wikipedia table's value to change to 'Yes', an editor would have to either erroneously claim Bexsero as a dedicated gonorrhea vaccine or prematurely add the GSK candidate. Given Wikipedia's typical moderation and the fact that the page survived the August news cycle without changing, the probability of the status changing to "Yes" in the next 1-3 months is very low. Following the instructions, uncertainty slightly increases for the later dates.
